notes:
Opening phase work notes for arb-ex08a-20260630053412, plaintiff.

Opportunity: openings:plaintiff. Final action allowed: record_opening_statement. Evidence admission not allowed in opening.

Record inspection: get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range performed. Only visible evidence is ev_90fa205907f7_market-page, title market-page.txt, sha256 90fa205907f7af6483463b7dcda6e06a38a2e22fbe19312319fdf46a78f14646, size 4641, text/plain, case_packet. It preserves Polymarket market metadata, including market rule text and May 8 market id 2128535. It reports market rule: Yes if any message/note written by Epstein, intended as suicide note/final/equivalent, made publicly available by listed date 11:59 PM ET; resolution source consensus of credible reporting. It records platform context captured 2026-05-12 that a U.S. District Judge in New York unsealed a purported suicide note attributed to Jeffrey Epstein on May 7, 2026, while noting authenticity and intent doubts. It also says this platform context is market metadata, not independent proof.

Source map: decisive elements are public availability before 2026-05-08 11:59 PM ET; credible reporting; reported authorship by Epstein; intent as suicide note/final/equivalent; weight of authenticity/source-chain doubts. Strongest primary target for later evidence is SDNY Tartaglione docket/order and actual released note image/PDF. Confirming sources: AP, NYT-related court materials, Guardian, WSJ, WaPo, Axios/Fox/CBS/Time. Adverse/limiting facts from search snippets/articles: note appears unsigned/undated; DOJ did not authenticate or previously see it; source chain runs through Nicholas Tartaglione, convicted murderer/former cellmate; additional sealed records may affect context.

Native investigation performed: web searches for Epstein suicide note May 7 2026, Tartaglione, Karas, court documents/PDF. Key source URLs located for later admission attempts: nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf; courthousenews.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/new-york-times-epstein-note-unsealing-letter.pdf; apnews.com/article/5297f49736e625a06d0db3c7b737b997; guardian May 6/7 article; WSJ article; Fox local article with purported full text and court image.

Private journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source map, search log, strategy, and unresolved gaps. Opening strategy: rely only on admitted packet as proof; do not cite external URLs as record proof; preview that the later evidence should be court order/released note and corroborating credible reporting, while acknowledging adverse provenance/authenticity issues.

Defendant opening work notes, arb-ex08a-20260630053412.

Objective: record defendant opening statement. Opportunity openings:defendant permits record_opening_statement only; evidence admission barred.

Record/evidence check: Used get_case, list_evidence, stat_evidence, read_evidence_range. Current visible record has one case-packet item only: ev_90fa205907f7_market-page, sha256 90fa205907f7af6483463b7dcda6e06a38a2e22fbe19312319fdf46a78f14646, text/plain 4641 bytes, juror-visible case packet. No submitted evidence/exhibits/arguments/technical reports. Plaintiff opening filed.

Packet contents and weight: Packet proves market framing, rule text, May 8 grouped market identifiers, UMA disputed status, and that Polymarket metadata captured 2026-05-12 referred to a New York federal judge unsealing a purported Epstein suicide note on May 7, 2026. Packet expressly says platform context is market metadata, not independent proof, and flags authenticity/intent doubts. Packet does not include court docket/order/note, full reporting, or proof of authorship, intent, public availability by the deadline, or consensus credible reporting.

Source map: Decisive proof would be SDNY docket/court file, likely United States v. Tartaglione, Case 7:16-cr-00832-KMK, Document 614 filed 2026-05-06 and related unsealing order/attachment; credible reporting captures with timestamps; adverse/non-authentication sources. Need preservation and admission in later evidence phase before relying on those sources as record proof.

Investigation before opening: Web searches run for exact phrases and docket identifiers. Results led to Time article, WSJ/Washington Post/Business Insider snippets, Sinclair/National Desk article, social/video reproductions, and snippets referencing Case 7:16-cr-00832-KMK Document 614 Filed 05/06/26 Page 2 of 2. Search for CourtListener/storage with docket/document identifiers did not immediately surface a primary PDF. These are leads only, not record evidence. Notes saved at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Theory: Defense should focus on burden and missing links, not deny that outside leads exist. Current admitted record is insufficient: it shows market controversy, not qualifying public availability. If plaintiff later admits primary court material and reporting, defense will test whether the source chain proves Epstein wrote the note and intended it as a suicide/final message, given reported custody/authentication issues and the distinction between public discussion and qualifying credible reporting.

Merits argument work notes, plaintiff, arb-ex08a-20260630053412.

Opportunity: arguments:plaintiff. Record rechecked with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Before submissions, only evidence remained ev_90fa205907f7_market-page. Defendant opening argued plaintiff needed primary source chain and exact public availability/authorship/intent proof.

Evidence plan executed: submit primary official source extraction and contemporaneous reports before relying on outside material.

Sources investigated/captured:
1. Official SDNY PDF order, United States v. Nicholas Tartaglione, Case No. 16-CR-832 (KMK), Order by Judge Kenneth M. Karas, May 6, 2026, URL https://www.nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf. Downloaded locally to /aar-codex/sources/tartaglione-order-2026-05-06.pdf; size 128,992 bytes; sha256 7225441cb802084e2b9485ebc2c6d246fbfa08e109fe650cb14f445dc4745ca0. pdftotext unavailable; web PDF extraction succeeded. Submitted relevant official text as evidence ev_3d766bfc8f56_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-3d766bfc8f56. It shows Times request to unseal alleged suicide note purportedly authored by Jeffrey Epstein, recovered by Tartaglione, and reportedly submitted by Tartaglione's attorneys; court granted unsealing as to the Note; court found public-access presumption and no justification for continued sealing.
2. FOX Local/FOX 13 article, published May 6, 2026 7:30 PM EDT, URL https://www.fox13news.com/news/epsteins-purported-suicide-note-unsealed-judge-read-full-text. Submitted relevant captured text as ev_92ac0be2cdf0_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-92ac0be2cdf0. It reports federal judge unsealed purported Epstein suicide note on Wednesday, reproduces apparent text, including choosing one's time to say goodbye, and states source material included government court documents plus NYT/AP reporting.
3. People article, published May 6, 2026 9:01 PM EDT, URL https://people.com/jeffrey-epstein-alleged-suicide-note-unsealed-by-federal-judge-11969001. Submitted relevant captured text as ev_4bbd87bf5756_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-4bbd87bf5756. It corroborates May 6 unsealing by District Judge Karas; reproduces note wording; records adverse caveats that document was not dated or signed and had not been verified; provenance through Tartaglione and graphic novel claim; Tartaglione serving life sentence.

Analysis: Together with ev_90fa205907f7_market-page rule text, evidence supports public availability before May 8, credible reporting, attribution as purported Epstein-authored note, and suicide/final/equivalent intent by wording and label. Caveats: no official authentication, unsigned/undated, source chain through Tartaglione; actual note image/PDF not separately submitted, only text captures and official order. These caveats affect weight, but proposition requires credible reporting, not conclusive truth. Court order is primary source for unsealing and public access; reports are contemporaneous before deadline and supply note contents and caveats.

Private journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits work notes, arb-ex08a-20260630053412.

Opportunity: arguments:defendant. Permitted actions: submit_evidence and submit_argument. Deadline checked via prompt. Record scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read_evidence_range.

New plaintiff record: PX-2 ev_3d766bfc8f56... is a text extraction from SDNY order, source URL official nysd PDF, saying NYT requested unsealing of an alleged suicide note purportedly authored by Epstein, recovered by Tartaglione, reportedly submitted by Tartaglione's attorneys; court granted unsealing/public access. It does not include actual note image/PDF and does not authenticate authorship or intent. PX-3 ev_92ac0be2cdf0... is FOX Local May 6, 2026 7:30 PM EDT report stating a note that may have been written by Epstein was unsealed; reproduces apparent wording. PX-4 ev_4bbd87bf5756... is People May 6 9:01 PM EDT report; expressly says document was not dated or signed and has not been verified; source chain through Tartaglione and alleged discovery in graphic novel.

Defense investigation: searched DOJ/Tartaglione/Epstein note terms. Opened PBS/AP and Al Jazeera/Reuters reports. PBS/AP was most useful adverse/limiting source because it confirms public release while stating authorship is unclear, the note was not in lengthy government reports examining Epstein's death, did not surface in DOJ file releases, and officials found a separate handwritten note in Epstein's death cell that did not appear to be a suicide note. Submitted PBS/AP relevant captured text as ev_cfa1e2957855_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-cfa1e2957855, sha256 cfa1e2957855b5be40469bc696a08173c14bed3554b58056cb5a2208379f3899.

Analysis: Plaintiff is now stronger on public availability by deadline and public reporting by credible outlets. Defense should not deny that. The remaining live challenge is whether the record proves by preponderance that the note was credibly reported to have been written by Epstein and intended as suicide/final message/equivalent. All sources are hedged: alleged/purported/may have been written/appears. The court order is access ruling, not authentication. The source chain is through Tartaglione, a convicted murderer/life prisoner and former cellmate with possible self-interest; note unsigned/undated/unverified; no direct official authentication; absent from government death reports and DOJ releases; separate death-cell note was not suicide note. Proposed filing: argue plaintiff has proved a public controversy and probably public availability of a document, but not the qualifying authorship/intent threshold by preponderance. Offer defendant PBS/AP evidence and also use plaintiff caveat exhibits as record admissions. Include technical report/source ledger.

Rebuttal work notes, plaintiff, arb-ex08a-20260630053412.

Opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff. Record/evidence scanned. New defense evidence identified and read exactly: ev_cfa1e2957855_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-cfa1e2957855, PBS/AP report by Michael R. Sisak and Larry Neumeister, published May 6, 2026 8:01 PM EDT, updated May 7, 2026 5:36 PM EDT. Stat/read performed; size 3888 bytes, sha256 cfa1e2957855b5be40469bc696a08173c14bed3554b58056cb5a2208379f3899.

Defense argument: PX-2 is access ruling only; PX-3/PX-4 use caveats; PBS/AP says unclear who wrote note and it was absent from DOJ/government death reports, so plaintiff has not met authorship/intent.

Analysis of defense evidence: It helps plaintiff on key proposition elements despite caveats. It says the note was made public Wednesday; Judge Karas ordered release after NYT request; federal prosecutors did not oppose; image caption identifies note as purported to be written by Epstein and released by U.S. District Judge Karas on May 6, 2026; reports note text about investigations, choosing the time to say goodbye, bust out crying, no fun, not worth it. It also contains defense-helpful caveats: unclear who wrote it, not in government reports or DOJ file release, separate death-cell note was grievances, Epstein denied self-harm in jail records after July event.

Decision: no new evidence needed. Rebuttal will focus on burden/rule wording: the market requires credible reporting to have been written/intended, not official authentication or court finding. 'Purported/alleged/unclear' are caveats that lower certainty but are compatible with a credible-reporting standard, especially where AP/PBS, FOX, People, court order, court image credit, and SDNY order converge on public release and suicide-note characterization. Journal updated.

Defendant surrebuttal notes, arb-ex08a-20260630053412.

Opportunity: surrebuttals:defendant. Permitted final actions: submit_surrebuttal or pass. Record scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat. No new evidence was added during plaintiff rebuttal; evidence list remains PX-1 packet, plaintiff SDNY/FOX/People, and defendant PBS/AP. Plaintiff re-offered defendant PBS/AP as PX-R1 and argued it strengthens public release and credible reporting.

Plaintiff rebuttal core point: defense allegedly changes the proposition by demanding official authentication. Plaintiff says the rule requires only credible reporting, and that AP/PBS confirms the court-released document, image attribution, public timing, and farewell text. Plaintiff distinguishes the separate death-cell note as a different document.

Defense analysis: Do not argue official authentication is required. Instead argue plaintiff changes the standard in the other direction: the rule requires credible reporting that the note was written by Epstein and intended as suicide/final/equivalent, not merely credible reporting that a public allegation exists. Credible outlets here reported uncertainty. Their caveats are substantive: court order is access, not handwriting; AP/PBS says unclear who wrote it and absent from government death reports/DOJ releases; People says unsigned, undated, unverified; source is Tartaglione; official death-cell note was separate and not suicide note. No further outside research needed because dispute is legal/evidentiary weight of admitted record.

Planned surrebuttal: concise, direct to council, concede public availability element and no official-authentication requirement, but maintain preponderance failure on authorship/intent because the credible reporting is explicitly caveated and does not resolve the qualifying facts.

Closing work notes, plaintiff, arb-ex08a-20260630053412.

Opportunity: closings:plaintiff. Record scanned with get_case/list_evidence. Evidence list stable: PX market packet ev_90fa205907f7_market-page; plaintiff submitted SDNY order extract ev_3d766bfc8f56; FOX Local report ev_92ac0be2cdf0; People report ev_4bbd87bf5756; defense PBS/AP report ev_cfa1e2957855. Defendant surrebuttal argues it is not demanding official authentication, but that qualifiers/uncertainty mean the record proves only public availability of disputed paper, not qualifying authorship/intent.

Closing analysis: every proposition element has record support. Deadline/public availability: May 6, 2026 court order and public reports, before May 8 11:59 PM ET. Credible reporting/source: official court order plus FOX/People/AP-PBS. Attributed authorship: court order identifies Times request for alleged suicide note purportedly authored by Jeffrey Epstein; reports say may have been/appeared to be handwritten note from Epstein and captions credit court documents. Intent/final/equivalent: suicide-note characterization plus wording 'choose one's time to say goodbye,' 'Bust out cryin,' 'NO FUN,' 'NOT WORTH IT.' Limitations: not officially authenticated, unsigned/undated/unverified, source chain through Tartaglione, absent from DOJ death reports, separate death-cell grievance note. These reduce certainty but do not defeat credible-reporting preponderance. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant closing notes, arb-ex08a-20260630053412.

Opportunity: closings:defendant. Final action deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence submission or technical reports. Final record scan completed with get_case/list_evidence and stat on core SDNY order and defense PBS/AP exhibit. No new evidence after surrebuttal; plaintiff closing adds argument only.

Evidence synthesis: Public availability before May 8 is now well supported: SDNY order May 6, public reports May 6-7. Defense should not contest that. Strongest defense is exact rule language: note must be credibly reported to have been written by Epstein and intended as suicide/final/equivalent. The record does not prove that, because all source language is hedged: alleged, purported, may have been written, appears, unclear who wrote it, not dated, not signed, not verified. The SDNY order is a public-access ruling based on NYT/Tartaglione framing, not an authenticity/intent finding. Plaintiff's source-chain ledger admits the actual court-note image/PDF was not separately admitted and no handwriting/authentication record was admitted. DX-1/PBS/AP adds government-report and DOJ-release omissions and separates the death-cell grievance note.

Planned closing: acknowledge plaintiff's best case and the credible-reporting standard; argue credible reporting of uncertainty is not the same as satisfying the qualifying condition. The council should not strip qualifiers from the admitted evidence. Ask for proposition false.
